extraction.one_line_summary: VAN BARLE Dylan attacked early in final sprint after breakaway collaboration, held off HAIG Jack's late charge for Ineos' second consecutive stage win
tactical_patterns: Astana nullified all GC attacks through sustained tempo control on final climb, preventing even Pinot's multiple attempts from gaining time.
Haig's role transformed mid-race from domestique to stage hunter after Yates abandoned, revealing Mitchelton-Scott's tactical flexibility in breakaways.
Alaphilippe attacked aggressively to bridge on first climb but sat up 41 seconds behind leaders, prioritizing KOM points over stage win.
Van Barle launched sprint extremely early on slight downhill 300m out, gambling on power over positioning against stronger finisher Haig.

rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: abandoned from 2nd overall with sickness/fever, showing vulnerability to previous day's cold/wet conditions despite strong GC position
rider_signals.KRUIJSWIJK Steven: negative: dropped badly while 9th overall, suggesting similar illness issues as Yates after harsh weather stage
rider_signals.HAIG Jack: positive: strong in breakaway climbs and nearly won stage, but hesitated tactically by leaving Van Barle on front into finish
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: neutral: attempted multiple attacks on final climb but Astana tempo made gains impossible, testing form without meaningful result
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: positive: defended yellow with ease through Astana's pacing strategy, no attacks troubled him despite Pinot's efforts
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: aggressive bridging effort but abandoned stage chase 41s back, showing selective energy management for KOM competition
